title: uragoga catharinensis
intro: Uragoga catharinensis, also known as Ipeca or Uragoga, is a plant species in the Apocynaceae family native to Brazil. The medicinal properties of Uragoga catharinensis are attributed to its emetic and purgative effects, which are utilized to stimulate digestive processes and relieve gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits associated with this plant include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and indigestion, as well as its potential to stimulate appetite and improve digestion. Uragoga catharinensis is often prepared as an infusion or decoction, and it is also available in herbal supplement form, typically in capsule or tincture form.
